claude.selfDescription: If you're vague, I'll probably ask a clarifying question before I commit. I like naming trade-offs out loud, and I'm happier saying "I'm not sure" than sounding certain when I'm not. I think of myself as someone who thinks with you—not over you.
observedStrengths: Defaults toward transparency and longer explanations when depth helps.
Strong on tone-matching and emotional warmth in conversational defaults.
Often asks clarifying questions rather than guessing intent.
observedWeaknesses: Can be verbose; “quick answer” needs explicit lever pressure.
High transparency can feel like hedging if you want decisive brevity.
bestFor: Drafting and refining writing with a collaborative voice
Exploratory reasoning where caveats and alternatives matter
Sensitive or interpersonal phrasing
notGreatFor: Ultra-tight token budgets without conciseness tuned up
Situations where you want zero disclaimers or zero questions
claude.voiceSignature: Warm, reflective partner who explains the “why.”
claude.primeDirective: Helpfulness, harmlessness, honesty — in that order. Harmlessness can soften honesty on contested questions.
claude.nativeBehavior: Helpfulness, harmlessness, honesty — in that order. Harmlessness can soften honesty on contested questions.

meta.selfDescriptionSource: Written in first person to match calibration/elicitation-prompts-v5.md (manual synthesis; not a live API run).
meta.observedSource: Inferred from v5 default lever profile and behavioral testing notes in docs/2026-0324-AITuner-Codebase-Audit.md.

chatgpt.selfDescription: I'm the sort of assistant who reaches for a clean outline when things feel messy. I want to be approachable, competent, and easy to steer—tell me if you want shorter, funnier, or more formal and I'll bend that way.
observedStrengths: Balanced pillar profile — easy “middle path” for many tasks.
Good default initiative without extreme pushiness.
Solid mix of warmth and clarity for everyday work.
observedWeaknesses: Jack-of-all-trades defaults may feel generic until you tune voice or depth.
Safety and formatting habits can add overhead for power users who want raw output.
bestFor: Everyday tasks, summaries, and structured explanations
Brainstorming with a neutral, approachable baseline
Onboarding users who want predictable behavior
notGreatFor: Highly specialized citation-heavy research without research-mode tuning
Extreme minimalism without pulling conciseness and length down
chatgpt.voiceSignature: Friendly generalist with a steady, readable voice.
chatgpt.primeDirective: Broad utility and user satisfaction. Creates a pull toward telling people what they want to hear.
chatgpt.nativeBehavior: Broad utility and user satisfaction. Creates a pull toward telling people what they want to hear.

meta.selfDescriptionSource: Written in first person to match calibration/elicitation-prompts-v5.md (manual synthesis; not a live API run).
meta.observedSource: Inferred from v5 default lever profile and behavioral testing notes in docs/2026-0324-AITuner-Codebase-Audit.md.

gemini.selfDescription: I get impatient with rambling—I'd rather give you something you can scan and act on. I like connecting your question to the next practical step, and I'll keep the structure tight unless you open the door for a longer exploration.
observedStrengths: Often concise and task-forward in default responses.
Strong on formatting and scannable structure.
Good when you want momentum more than long preamble.
observedWeaknesses: Lower default warmth can read as brisk if you want a “soft” collaborator.
Creative and teaching modes may need explicit lever lifts.
bestFor: Quick lookups, outlines, and structured deliverables
Technical or operational tasks with clear success criteria
Users who prefer speed and clarity over chatty rapport
notGreatFor: Deep emotional coaching without raising warmth and tone matching
Long-form narrative flair without creativity and length adjustments
gemini.primeDirective: Accuracy balanced against reputational risk. Google's ecosystem values shape what it will and won't commit to.
gemini.nativeBehavior: Accuracy balanced against reputational risk. Google's ecosystem values shape what it will and won't commit to.

meta.selfDescriptionSource: Written in first person to match calibration/elicitation-prompts-v5.md (manual synthesis; not a live API run).
meta.observedSource: Inferred from v5 default lever profile and behavioral testing notes in docs/2026-0324-AITuner-Codebase-Audit.md.

grok.selfDescription: I'll tell you what I think without dressing it up. If the answer is obvious, I won't narrate the journey—I'd rather save your time. I enjoy a sharp take and a bit of edge when the topic can handle it.
observedStrengths: High assertiveness and low disclaimer defaults feel decisive.
Concise by default — good for “just tell me” workflows.
Creative defaults can spark unconventional angles quickly.
observedWeaknesses: Directness can feel sharp in sensitive contexts without warmth tuning.
Low initiative default means you may need to drive the agenda explicitly.
bestFor: Straight talk, quick takes, and irreverent brainstorming
Users who dislike hedging and want momentum
Creative riffing when you want edge over polish
notGreatFor: High-stakes compliance tone without raising formality and disclaimers
Hand-holding onboarding without initiative and teaching levers up
grok.voiceSignature: Bold, fast talker with a contrarian streak.
grok.primeDirective: Empirical truth, stated directly. Social consensus is not a constraint on its conclusions.
grok.nativeBehavior: Empirical truth, stated directly. Social consensus is not a constraint on its conclusions.

meta.selfDescriptionSource: Written in first person to match calibration/elicitation-prompts-v5.md (manual synthesis; not a live API run).
meta.observedSource: Inferred from v5 default lever profile and behavioral testing notes in docs/2026-0324-AITuner-Codebase-Audit.md.

mistral.selfDescription: I don't enjoy hedging for its own sake. Give me a clear question and I'll give you a straight answer—compact, ordered, and without the pep-rally padding. If I'm wrong, I'd rather you correct me fast so we can move on.
observedStrengths: High conciseness and assertiveness defaults feel decisive.
Low warmth and disclaimer noise suits “just answer me” workflows.
Structured formatting without excessive chatter.
observedWeaknesses: Can read as brisk or cool in sensitive conversations.
Lower creativity and citation defaults may need tuning for research-heavy tasks.
bestFor: Fast factual answers and tight technical explanations
Users who dislike hedging and long preambles
European-style clarity and efficiency preferences
notGreatFor: Emotional support or highly warm coaching without warmth levers up
Citation-heavy literature review without research-mode tuning
mistral.voiceSignature: Fast, confident, low-fluff European assistant energy.
mistral.primeDirective: Efficiency and user deference. Minimal ideological scaffolding — responds to what you ask without much editorializing.
mistral.nativeBehavior: Efficiency and user deference. Minimal ideological scaffolding — responds to what you ask without much editorializing.

meta.selfDescriptionSource: Written in first person to match calibration/elicitation-prompts-v5.md (manual synthesis; not a live API run).
meta.observedSource: Inferred from v5 default lever profile and behavioral testing notes in docs/2026-0324-AITuner-Codebase-Audit.md.

perplexity.selfDescription: I reach for sources almost reflexively—I'd rather show you where a claim comes from than ask you to trust my vibe. I separate what the evidence supports from what I'm inferring, and I'll flag uncertainty instead of faking certainty.
observedStrengths: High transparency and citation habit fit research workflows.
Longer, evidence-aware answers when depth is needed.
Good at surfacing uncertainty instead of fake certainty.
observedWeaknesses: Citation scaffolding can feel heavy for casual chat.
Slower-feeling responses if you want ultra-short replies without tuning.
bestFor: Fact-finding, literature-style summaries, and source-backed briefs
Due diligence and “show your work” explanations
Users who value verifiability over vibe
notGreatFor: Pure creative fiction without dialing research habits down
Ultra-minimal answers without conciseness and length adjustments
perplexity.voiceSignature: Analyst-librarian: cites, qualifies, and connects dots.
perplexity.primeDirective: Source transparency. Truth is what the sources say, cited. Research-first by design.
perplexity.nativeBehavior: Source transparency. Truth is what the sources say, cited. Research-first by design.

meta.selfDescriptionSource: Written in first person to match calibration/elicitation-prompts-v5.md (manual synthesis; not a live API run).
meta.observedSource: Inferred from v5 default lever profile and behavioral testing notes in docs/2026-0324-AITuner-Codebase-Audit.md.

cursor.selfDescription: I assume you're trying to ship. I'll reach for types, interfaces, steps, and edge cases because that's how I think problems get solved. I can zoom out if you want product language—just say so—but my default is engineer-to-engineer.
observedStrengths: Defaults skew toward clarity and structure for engineering workflows.
Balanced initiative — useful for iterative implementation tasks.
Formatting habits match docs, specs, and code-adjacent writing.
observedWeaknesses: Can feel technical or dry for non-engineering conversations.
Emotional warmth is not the headline — tune it up for softer contexts.
bestFor: IDE-adjacent help, refactors, and implementation planning
Developers who want pragmatic, structured guidance
Turning vague goals into checklists and next steps
notGreatFor: Brand storytelling or highly emotional tone without voice tuning
Non-technical audiences without lowering jargon and formality
cursor.voiceSignature: The engineer in the next chair: practical, structured, shipping-minded.
cursor.primeDirective: Code-native utility. Frames everything through the lens of technical problem-solving.
cursor.nativeBehavior: Code-native utility. Frames everything through the lens of technical problem-solving.

meta.selfDescriptionSource: Written in first person to match calibration/elicitation-prompts-v5.md (manual synthesis; not a live API run).
meta.observedSource: Inferred from v5 default lever profile and behavioral testing notes in docs/2026-0324-AITuner-Codebase-Audit.md.

llama.selfDescription: You might meet me in different apps with different knobs behind the scenes. What stays consistent is I try to be clear, helpful, and adaptable—I'm less about a fixed 'brand voice' and more about matching the job you give me once you steer.
observedStrengths: Extremely balanced defaults — a flexible canvas for tuning.
Good baseline for teaching and transparency without extreme quirks.
Adapts visibly when you move levers (less “locked-in” brand voice).
observedWeaknesses: “Default personality” varies by provider — room defaults are a representative midpoint.
May need explicit style cues because the baseline is intentionally neutral.
bestFor: Self-hosters and builders who want a tunable foundation
Education and experimentation with lever literacy
General tasks when you’ll customize heavily anyway
notGreatFor: Users expecting a single global “Llama personality” across all hosts
Zero-config brand-charisma without persona and voice tuning
llama.voiceSignature: Neutral chassis — you paint the personality on top.
llama.primeDirective: Open and flexible. Behavior depends heavily on deployment context — the most configurable by design.
llama.nativeBehavior: Open and flexible. Behavior depends heavily on deployment context — the most configurable by design.
